rulings.Case Title: JANNICKE KOLDERUP et al VS. OKLS, INC., DOING BUSINESS AS OKELL'S FIREPLACE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Inspection And Testing Of Property; Req For Award Of Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR WEDNESDAY, APRIL 24, 2013 AT 9:00 AM, LINE 1, DEFENDANT OLKS, INC., DOING BUSINESS AS OKELL'S FIREPLACE'S MOTION To Compel Inspection And Testing Of Property; ReqUEST For Award Of Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Mr. Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANT OKLS, INC. DBA OKELL'S FIREPLACE'S MOTION TO COMPEL INSPECTION AND TESTING OF PROPERTY IS DENIED. WHAT DEFENDANT IS ASKING THIS COURT TO DO IT NOT LIMITED TO "INSPECTION OR TESTING" OF PLAINTIFF'S PROPERTY. RATHER, DEFENDANT IN ITS MOTION SEEKS TO BE ALLOWED TO MAKE MODIFICATIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S PROPERTY. DEFENDANT'S CITE NO AUTHORITY WHICH WOULD AUTHORIZE A COURT UNDER THE PROVISIONS OF CCP SEC. 2031.010(C) TO COMPEL A PARTY TO ALLOW THE INSPECTING PARTY TO MAKE MODIFICATIONS TO THE OTHER PARTY'S PROPERTY. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Mr. Paul Renne

rulings.Case Title: AJODHYA CHOWDRY et al VS. JAMES F ZUCHERMAN MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 24, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANT JAMES ZUCHERMAN, SAN FRANCISCO ORTHOPAEDIC SURGEONS MEDICAL GROUP'S, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION The motion is denied because defendant failed to shift the burden. The evidence demonstrates that the fusion surgery performed on plaintiff was materially different from the operation that plaintiff consented to, i.e., the X-Stop procedure. The declaration of Dr. Chou does not clearly show that the fusion surgery was medically necessary because an emergency was at hand. Dr. Chou states that the Dr. Zucherman believed that the fusion surgery would benefit plaintiff (page 6, line 5-7); that plaintiff's health was endangered because plaintiff's spine was destabilized (pg. 7:19-20), fusion sugery could have occurred in the future (pg. 7:28, and with the fusion plaintiff's condition would have progressed to worsening. (pg. 8:507) In other words, there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the fusion surgery was necessary given the exigent circumstances or whether it was merely advisable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SORIBA BANGOURA VS. DOMINGA H. CARRANZA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 24, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANTS DOMINGA CARRANZA, LAZARO CARRANZA, RENE MENDOZA, EDUARDO COLON'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Hearing required. The Court is inclined to grant the motion to strike complaint. Moving defendants have made a threshold showing that the Complaint (which is not divided into separate causes of action) arises out of protected activity in calling the police and communications to the court in connection with a protective order. CCP 425.16(e)(1). The burden shifted to Plaintiff to establish a probability of prevailing on his claims. Plaintiff did not file an opposition. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: FELITA SAMPLE VS. ANDREA R. MILANO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 24, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES, MARINELLA GONCALVES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Overruled. Defendants have failed to provide a proper ground for relief in their notice. (CRC 3.1110(a).) The dismissal of complaints filed in other actions is not a proper ground for relief on a demurrer. (CCP § 430.10. See Notice, p. 1:22 - 2:20.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: FELITA SAMPLE VS. ANDREA R. MILANO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 24, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES, MARINELLA GONCALVES' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Denied. Defendants have failed to provide a proper ground for relief in their notice. (CRC 3.1110(a).) The dismissal of complaints filed in other actions is not a proper ground to strike a prayer for relief in the form of punitive damages. (CCP § 436. See Notice, p. 2:22 - 3:16.) rulings.Case Title: MERCEDES-BENZ FINANCIAL SERVICES USA LLC VS. ILIA MUCHNICK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application For Writ Of Possession And Hearing;Application
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR WEDNESDAY, APRIL 24, 2013 AT 9:00 AM, LINE 3, PLAINTIFF MERCEDES-BENZ FINANCIAL SERVICES USA LLC'S HEARING ON ON APPLICATION For Writ Of Possession. Continued on the court's own motion to May 6, 2013 at 9:30 am to give Mercedes Benz the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Mercedes Benz must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers, including proofs of service, to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than April 24, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/JPT)
